visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa allows you to stay and receive medical care within the Schengen territory as stated on your visa. To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least 3 to 4 working weeks before the intended date of departure.

## Visa Fees

[bel > sau > en > Table:Medical visit > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/Card.

## VFS SERVICE CHARGE
Applicants applying at the Belgium Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of SAR 151.2 (VAT Included) payable in cash
Please Note: 
- The applicable       visa fee in Saudi Riyal is as per the current exchange rate. The same is       subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee       & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
Children below the age of 6 years on the date of submission at  VFS are exempted of the Visa Fee. However VFS service charges will be  applicable.
Below are the additional optional services available at the visa  application centre at nominal price.

[bel > sau > en > Table:Medical visit > Visa Fees 2]
All optional services are non-refundable.
The visa application should be submitted during (12 months) following the decision of the CGRS to recognize refugee status or to grant subsidiary protection the original documents + 1 copy of the following documents (the originals will be returned to you).

## Required Documents

[bel > sau > en > Table:Medical visit > Required Documents 1]

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.
Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye
Glasses and head covers

If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
Head coverings : 
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
Expression and frame

Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Normal processing time of a short stay visa application during this season might take up to 30 working days as below: 
- Processing time counts from the day of receiving the application at the Embassy.
- Processing time does not include official holidays and weekends.
- Submitting an incomplete application will result in longer processing time and a higher chance of a negative decision.
- This period may, in individual cases, be extended if more detailed examination of your application and/or additional documents are required.
 If you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Dammam, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics. 
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.

## Legalization
Legalizations of the documents for long stay
- Legalization of the documents can be submitted at Belgium Embassy in Riyadh on every Monday between 13:00 – 15:00 hrs and the legalized documents can be collected on the following Thursday between 13:00 – 14:00 hrs.
- Original documents and the translations of them must be legalized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to be legalized by the Belgium Embassy
- Documents legalization required for the long stay
- Medical Report from an authorised hospital approved by the Ministry of Health in Saudi Arabia
- Criminal Report from Police station (Applicants above 18 years)
- Birth Certificate
- Marriage Certificate
- If going for studies; the sponsor must get the document attested by Chamber of Commerce and legalized by Ministry of Foreign Affairs

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 This visa allows you to stay and travel within the Schengen territory as stated on your visa. You may enter the Schengen territory as a visitor, but you are not allowed to take up work. To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least three working weeks before the intended date of departure. Please note you can submit your visa application up to 6 months prior to your date of travel 
Basic Requirements: 
To ensure application is processed without any delay, following a documents have to be included. 
- Complete & Signed visa application form
- Supporting documentation as per relevant checklist
Applicants are requested to read carefully the list of required documents that needs to be submitted together with their visa application. Please make sure that you have all the documents indicated in the list, since an incomplete file can lead to the refusal of your visa.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your  fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months,  you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that  the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be  asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this  possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be  taken again.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 The Business/Meeting/Training/Trade Fair Visa for the Schengen Countries is valid for a short stay (maximum 90 days) This visa allows you to stay and travel within the Schengen territory as stated on your visa. You may enter the Schengen
                        territory as a visitor or for business consultations, but you are not allowed to take up work.
Applicant that is going to buy goods in Belgium (like second hand cars) he will apply for a commercial visa. While applicant that works for organisation and is going to do training at their organisation in Belgium, will apply for a
                        professional visa.
To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least three working weeks before the intended date of departure. Please note you can submit your visa application up to 3 months prior to your date
                        of travel.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the
                        right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Overview
To ensure application is processed without any delay, following are the documents to be included.
- Complete & Signed visa application form.
- Original Passport & three (3) clear copies of applicant’s current passport datapage.
- Supporting documentation as per the relevant checklist.
Applicants are requested to read carefully the list of required documents that need to be submitted together with their visa application. Please make sure that you have all the documents indicated in the list, since an incomplete file can lead to the refusal of your visa.To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least three working weeks before the intended date of departure. Please note you can submit your visa application up to 3 months prior to your date of travel.
In case you have already submitted your  fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months,  you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your  fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months,  you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that  the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be  asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this  possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be  taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > sau > en > Table:EU Member (Spouse Or Children) > Visa Fees 1]
The above mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Cash/Card. 

## VFS SERVICE CHARGE
Applicants applying at the Belgium Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of SAR 151.2 (VAT Included) payable in cash
Please Note: 
- The applicable       visa fee in Saudi Riyal is as per the current exchange rate. The same is       subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee       & service charges is non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
Children below the age of 6 years on the date of submission at  VFS are exempted of the Visa Fee. However VFS service charges will be  applicable.
Below are the additional optional services available at the visa  application centre at nominal price.

[bel > sau > en > Table:EU Member (Spouse Or Children) > Visa Fees 2]
All optional services are non-refundable.
The visa application should be submitted during (12 months) following the decision of the CGRS to recognize refugee status or to grant subsidiary protection the original documents + 1 copy of the following documents (the originals will be returned to you).

## Required Documents

[bel > sau > en > Table:EU Member (Spouse Or Children) > Required Documents 1]

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.
Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye
Glasses and head covers

If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
Head coverings : 
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
Expression and frame

Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Normal processing time of a short stay visa application during this season might take up to 30 working days as below: 
- Processing time counts from the day of receiving the application at the Embassy.
- Processing time does not include official holidays and weekends.
- Submitting an incomplete application will result in longer processing time and a higher chance of a negative decision.
- This period may, in individual cases, be extended if more detailed examination of your application and/or additional documents are required.
 If you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Dammam, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics. 
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.

Overview
 The Business/Meeting/Training/Trade Fair Visa for the Schengen Countries is valid for a short stay (maximum 90 days) This visa allows you to stay and travel within the Schengen territory as stated on your visa. You may enter the Schengen
                        territory as a visitor or for business consultations, but you are not allowed to take up work.
Applicant that is going to buy goods in Belgium (like second hand cars) he will apply for a commercial visa. While applicant that works for organisation and is going to do training at their organisation in Belgium, will apply for a
                        professional visa.
To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least three working weeks before the intended date of departure. Please note you can submit your visa application up to 3 months prior to your date
                        of travel.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the
                        right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.
Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye
Glasses and head covers

If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
Head coverings : 
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.
Expression and frame

Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Normal processing time of a short stay visa application during this season might take up to 30 working days as below: 
- Processing time counts from the day of receiving the application at the Embassy.
- Processing time does not include official holidays and weekends.
- Submitting an incomplete application will result in longer processing time and a higher chance of a negative decision.
- This period may, in individual cases, be extended if more detailed examination of your application and/or additional documents are required.
 If you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Dammam, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics. 
Please note that your passport will be kept during the visa application process.
